bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with all valueC (worst for me, likely best for them). This is t=0.00, so I should open with my ideal bid - all valueA gives me utility of 1.0. There's no reason to concede at all this early, especially since the opponent just made their opening maximalist offer.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating their all-valueC offer (utility ~0.2 for me) with no concession at all. It's very early (t=0.02), so I have no reason to concede significantly. I'll maintain my high-utility position with all-valueA, which gives me maximum utility of 1.0. The opponent hasn't shown any flexibility, so I should hold firm and signal my position clearly.

The opponent is stubbornly offering all valueC (minimum utility for me = 0.2 on all issues), which gives me only 0.2 utility total. It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay near my optimal bid. I'll make a very minor concession on the least important issues (D, E, F) by offering valueB there, while holding firm on the high-weight issues (A, B, C). This signals willingness to negotiate while protecting my core interests. My utility: 0.3*1 + 0.2*1 + 0.2*1 + 0.1*0.61 + 0.1*0.61 + 0.1*0.61 ≈ 0.883.

The opponent is stubbornly holding at all-valueC (utility ~0.2 for me) with no concession whatsoever. It's still very early (t=0.06), so I should stay near my top utility while showing slight movement. I'll keep my high-weight issues (A, B, C) at valueA and concede the low-weight issues (D, E, F) to valueB, which I already did last turn. To avoid repeating exactly, I'll try conceding issueF to valueC (lowest weight issue) while keeping others strong.
